 {"id":"frankenredis-1ng7","title":"Implement command propagation from primary to connected replicas","description":"# Command Propagation to Replicas

## What
When FrankenRedis is acting as a primary and replicas are connected, write commands must be propagated to all connected replicas in real-time.

## Current State
The fr-server has `replication_follow_up_bytes()` which serializes commands for replication. Replica connections are tracked. The gap is that write commands are not actually pushed to replica write buffers after execution, and the backlog buffer is not populated.

## Implementation
1. After each write command execution in the main event loop, check if there are connected replicas
2. Serialize the command as RESP (already done by replication_follow_up_bytes)
3. Push the serialized bytes into each replica connection write_buf AND the backlog buffer
4. Advance replication offset by the number of bytes pushed
5. Handle slow replicas: if write_buf exceeds output_buffer_limit, disconnect replica with warning
6. Ensure read-only commands (GET, KEYS, etc.) are NOT propagated

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: Write command increments replication offset
- Test 2: Read command does NOT increment replication offset
- Test 3: Offset advances by exact RESP-serialized byte count
- Test 4: MULTI/EXEC propagates all commands in the transaction

## Integration Tests
- Test 5: Start primary, connect replica, SET 1000 keys on primary, verify all 1000 appear on replica
- Test 6: Slow replica (inject delay) — verify primary disconnects it after buffer overflow
- Test 7: Multiple replicas — verify all receive the same commands
- Test 8: INFO replication on primary shows connected_slaves count

## Logging
- Log propagation: "REPL: propagating SET key (42 bytes) to 2 replicas"
- Log offset advancement
- Log slow replica disconnection with reason

## Depends On
- REPLICAOF TCP client (1v8b) — need a connection to propagate to
- Backlog buffer (iz1j) — propagated bytes go here for partial resync
- RDB snapshot streaming (de0t) — replica must have initial state before streaming

## Files
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (propagation after command dispatch)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (offset tracking)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:16.983808258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:38.898911059Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:54:38.898680643Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: propagate_writes_to_replicas() at main.rs:2255 pushes AOF-serialized writes to all connected replica write buffers. Called in main event loop at line 534 after every command batch. Unit test at line 3564 verifies the flow.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-1v8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:44.677262603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-de0t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:14:57.688577941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-1ng7","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-iz1j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:13.790873067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-1qdg","title":"Implement real DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, RELOAD, and OBJECT subcommands","description":"# Implement real DEBUG subcommands

## What
Three DEBUG subcommands currently return OK without doing anything:
1. DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0/1: should toggle the active-expire cycle on/off
2. DEBUG RELOAD: should save + reload the dataset (equivalent to SAVE + restart load)
3. DEBUG OBJECT <key>: should return encoding, refcount, serializedlength, lru info

## Implementation

### DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
- Add pub active_expire_enabled: bool to Store (default true)
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 sets it to false, 1 sets it to true
- The runtime's active_expire_cycle checks this flag before running
- dispatch_argv needs &mut Store access (already has it)

### DEBUG RELOAD
- This is a runtime-level operation (needs to call persist_snapshot_to_disk then load_aof)
- In fr-command, we can't do this. Two options:
  a) Add a flag to Store (debug_reload_requested) that the runtime checks after dispatch
  b) Accept that DEBUG RELOAD only works from the runtime path (not from Lua)
- Recommendation: option (a) — add the flag, runtime checks it after each command batch

### DEBUG OBJECT <key>
- dispatch_argv has access to Store, which has object_encoding()
- Return format: \"Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<encoding> serializedlength:<len> lru:<idle> lru_seconds_idle:<idle_s>\"
- encoding: from store.object_encoding()
- serializedlength: from store.memory_usage_for_key()
- lru/idle: from store.object_idletime()
- refcount: always 1 (same as Redis for non-shared objects)

### DEBUG JMAP
- Keep as no-op — JVM thread dump is N/A for Rust. Return OK.

## Testing
- Unit test: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0, verify active_expire_enabled is false
- Unit test: DEBUG OBJECT <key> returns correct encoding string
- Conformance: add DEBUG OBJECT cases

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (debug_cmd)
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (active_expire_enabled field)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check active_expire_enabled, check debug_reload_requested)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:20:07.010370056Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267730522Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:15:56.267460231Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
